patient.name: Liam Johnson
patient.medical_history: Occasional alcohol use, previously treated for high cholesterol

timeline.summary: Triaged as SEMI_URGENT. Patient experiencing unusual fatigue and mild abdominal discomfort. Sent to doctor for further evaluation.

details.triage_reasoning: Patient reports unusual fatigue, mild abdominal discomfort, and nausea, indicative of potential underlying issues due to treatment history and current symptoms. These warrant further evaluation but are not critically life-threatening.
details.notes: Monitor for any signs of deterioration or new symptoms.
details.context_for_next: Patient requires further evaluation based on current symptoms and medication history.
details.viewer_output: Triaged as SEMI_URGENT. Patient experiencing unusual fatigue and mild abdominal discomfort. Sent to doctor for further evaluation.

timeline.summary: Ordering abdominal ultrasound from General Lab due to unexplained fatigue, abdominal discomfort, and nausea. Found 3 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

details.context_for_next: suspected abdominal or systemic issues based on symptoms and experiences
details.reasoning: Patient presents with unusual fatigue, mild abdominal discomfort, and nausea. Similar cases indicate abdominal issues like acute pancreatitis and gastroenteritis. Also, relevant experiences suggest an ultrasound is necessary to evaluate for underlying problems.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: Gastroenteritis and case #2: Acute Pancreatitis showed successful outcomes in similar cases.
details.viewer_output: Ordering abdominal ultrasound from General Lab due to unexplained fatigue, abdominal discomfort, and nausea. Found 3 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

timeline.summary: Ultrasound completed. Results will be reviewed by your doctor.

details.test_outcome: Liver size: 16 cm, Liver echogenicity: Increased, Splenomegaly: Yes, Ascites: Trace
details.viewer_output: Ultrasound completed. Results will be reviewed by your doctor.

timeline.summary: Diagnosis: Alcoholic liver disease with consequences of splenomegaly and trace ascites. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Advise abstinence from alcohol and monitor liver function.

details.diagnosis: Alcoholic liver disease with consequences of splenomegaly and trace ascites.
details.recommended_treatment: Advise abstinence from alcohol, monitor liver function, and potentially consider further evaluation with liver function tests.
details.context_for_next: Potential progression from minor ascites and splenomegaly to significant liver-related complications.
details.reasoning: Diagnosis based on increased liver size and echogenicity, along with splenomegaly and trace ascites, correlates with findings from past similar cases. I found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes that led to diagnoses of hepatobiliary conditions. Treatment reflects learned principles regarding abstinence and monitoring for liver disease.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: Mild Gastroenteritis yielding a beneficial outcome after appropriate treatment led me to consider corroborating liver-related symptoms with lifestyle modifications.
details.viewer_output: Diagnosis: Alcoholic liver disease with consequences of splenomegaly and trace ascites. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Advise abstinence from alcohol and monitor liver function.

timeline.summary: Learned: Fatigue with abdominal discomfort and splenomegaly requires HCV testing to rule out hepatitis C. This will improve future liver disease diagnoses.

details.principle_text: Fatigue with abdominal discomfort and splenomegaly requires HCV testing to rule out hepatitis C.
details.reasoning: The initial focus on alcohol-related liver damage overlooked viral hepatitis, which can present similarly and should be evaluated in the context of liver dysfunction.
details.viewer_output: Learned: Fatigue with abdominal discomfort and splenomegaly requires HCV testing to rule out hepatitis C. This will improve future liver disease diagnoses.
